text: I feel a sense of isolation as I enter this dimly-lit, stark corridor. The emptiness and stillness amplify each of my steps, and my mind drifts to existential questions about presence and absence—why do spaces built for people seem so foreign in their emptiness? The subtle glow of exit signs and the industrial fixtures remind me of the human effort to bring order, yet in the quiet, the building's utility becomes a silent testament to our need for structure and safety.
